verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is secreted by the epididymal epithelium into the epididymal lumen and then binds stably to the dorsal/postacrosomal region of the sperm plasma membrane during epididymal transit. This attachment is acquired post-translationally in a specialized secretory compartment and is wash-resistant enough to persist through capacitation, with CRISP1 functioning at the sperm surface during sperm-egg fusion. Not a TM/GPI protein itself, but stably anchored to a sperm-surface partner — fits stable_surface_attachment. Restricted to the male germline lineage, but the dominant mechanism is post-translational tethering to a surface partner rather than its own anchor.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Wash-resistance vs. equilibrium of CRISP1's non-covalent association with sperm surface partners is not fully characterized; could alternatively fit tissue_restricted_surface.
